summary: There is no established medical association between bobble-head doll syndrome and hydrocephalus; though both may involve third ventricular dilation observed in some neuroanatomical abnormalities, such findings are incidental and do not indicate a direct pathological or diagnostic relationship. This phenomenon remains unvalidated by mainstream medicine and should be considered clinically insignificant without further research.
